master_row.title: Buckingham Π-Theorem Application
master_row.canonical_statement: Dimensional analysis applied to gyromagnetic systems with fundamental quantities mass M, length R, time period T, magnetic field B, magnetic moment mu, electrical conductivity sigma and viscosity eta yields dimensionless groups including the magnetic Reynolds number R\_m and Elsasser number Lambda.
master_row.formation_note: Documented in the source as an algebraic definition relating σ, ω, v, L.
master_row.dictionary: Dimensional analysis applied to gyromagnetic systems with fundamental quantities mass M, length R, time period T, magnetic field B, magnetic moment mu, electrical conductivity sigma and viscosity eta yields dimensionless groups including the magnetic Reynolds number R_m and Elsasser number Lambda【31
master_row.encyclopedia: The Π‑theorem identifies the independent dimensionless parameters governing gyromagnetic dynamics. It shows that, despite many physical quantities, the behaviour is controlled by a small set of numbers. Interpretive role: This entry specifies the structure within the registry.
